rules.label: Eligible data breach definition
rules.obligation: An eligible data breach happens if there is unauthorised access to, unauthorised disclosure of, or loss of, personal information held by an entity, and the access, disclosure or loss is likely to result in serious harm to any of the individuals to whom the information relates.
rules.scope: Entities holding personal information under the NDB scheme

rules.label: Obligation to notify eligible data breach
rules.obligation: An entity must give a notification if it has reasonable grounds to believe that an eligible data breach has happened, or if it is directed to do so by the Commissioner.
rules.scope: Entities subject to the NDB scheme

rules.label: Commissioner may obtain information or documents
rules.obligation: The Commissioner may obtain information or documents in relation to actual or suspected eligible data breaches.
rules.scope: Office of the Australian Information Commissioner

rules.label: Entity includes file number recipient
rules.obligation: For the purposes of this Part, the term 'entity' is to be construed as including a person who is a file number recipient.
rules.scope: Definitional provision applying to Part IIIC of the Privacy Act 1988 (NDB scheme).

rules.label: Deemed holding of personal information disclosed to overseas recipient
rules.obligation: Where an APP entity has disclosed personal information about individuals to an overseas recipient, APP 8.1 applied to that disclosure, and the overseas recipient holds the information, this Part has effect as if the information were held by the APP entity and the APP entity were required under section 15 not to breach APP 11.1 in relation to the information.
rules.scope: APP entities that have disclosed personal information to overseas recipients under APP 8.1

rules.label: Deemed holding of credit eligibility information disclosed to bodies with no Australian link
rules.obligation: Where a credit provider has disclosed credit eligibility information about individuals to a related body corporate, body or person with no Australian link (under paragraph 21G(3)(b) or (c) or subsection 21M(1)) and that recipient holds the information, this Part has effect as if the information were held by the credit provider and the credit provider were required to comply with subsection 21S(1) in relation to the information.
rules.scope: Credit providers that have disclosed credit eligibility information to bodies or persons with no Australian link

rules.label: Exception for My Health Records Act notification
rules.obligation: This Part (NDB scheme) does not apply in relation to the access, disclosure or loss if it has been, or is required to be, notified under section 75 of the My Health Records Act 2012.
rules.scope: Information incidents already covered by My Health Records Act 2012 notification requirements

rules.label: Eligible data breach via unauthorised access or disclosure with likely serious harm
rules.obligation: Where there is unauthorised access to or unauthorised disclosure of the information and a reasonable person would conclude the access or disclosure would be likely to result in serious harm to any affected individual, this constitutes an eligible data breach and the individual is at risk from it.
rules.scope: APP entities, credit reporting bodies, credit providers and file number recipients holding relevant personal/credit/tax file number information

rules.label: Eligible data breach via loss likely to result in unauthorised access/disclosure and serious harm
rules.obligation: Where information is lost in circumstances where unauthorised access or disclosure is likely to occur and, assuming such access or disclosure occurred, a reasonable person would conclude it would be likely to result in serious harm to any affected individual, this constitutes an eligible data breach and the individual is at risk from it.
rules.scope: APP entities, credit reporting bodies, credit providers and file number recipients holding relevant personal/credit/tax file number information

rules.label: Remedial action for access/disclosure prevents eligible data breach
rules.obligation: Where an APP entity takes remedial action in relation to an unauthorised access or disclosure before it results in serious harm, and as a result a reasonable person would conclude the access or disclosure would not be likely to result in serious harm to any affected individual, the access or disclosure is taken never to have been an eligible data breach.
rules.scope: APP entities, credit reporting bodies, credit providers, file number recipients

rules.label: Remedial action for access/disclosure removes notification requirement for particular individual
rules.obligation: Where remedial action is taken before serious harm results to a particular individual, and as a result a reasonable person would conclude the access or disclosure would not be likely to result in serious harm to that individual, this Part does not require notifying that individual of the statement contents relating to the access or disclosure.
rules.scope: APP entities, credit reporting bodies, credit providers, file number recipients

rules.label: Remedial action for loss before unauthorised access/disclosure prevents eligible data breach
rules.obligation: Where a loss of information is covered and the entity takes action before there is any unauthorised access to or disclosure of the information, and as a result there is no unauthorised access or disclosure, the loss is taken never to have been an eligible data breach.
rules.scope: APP entities, credit reporting bodies, credit providers, file number recipients

rules.label: Remedial action for loss after unauthorised access/disclosure prevents eligible data breach
rules.obligation: Where a loss of information is covered and the entity takes action after unauthorised access or disclosure occurs but before it results in serious harm, and as a result a reasonable person would conclude the access or disclosure would not be likely to result in serious harm to any affected individual, the loss is taken never to have been an eligible data breach.
rules.scope: APP entities, credit reporting bodies, credit providers, file number recipients

rules.label: Remedial action for loss removes notification requirement for particular individual
rules.obligation: Where a loss is covered and the entity takes action after unauthorised access or disclosure but before serious harm results to a particular individual, and as a result a reasonable person would conclude the access or disclosure would not be likely to result in serious harm to that individual, this Part does not require notifying that individual of the statement contents relating to the loss.
rules.scope: APP entities, credit reporting bodies, credit providers, file number recipients

rules.label: Relevant matters for assessing likelihood of serious harm
rules.obligation: In determining whether a reasonable person would conclude that access to or disclosure of information would, or would not, be likely to result in serious harm to affected individuals, have regard to: the kind(s) of information; its sensitivity; whether it is protected by security measures; the likelihood those measures could be overcome; the persons or kinds of persons who have obtained or could obtain the information; where security technology was designed to make information unintelligible, the likelihood that persons with intent to harm could obtain knowledge required to circumvent it; the nature of the harm; and any other relevant matters.
rules.scope: Assessment of serious harm under Part IIIC (NDB scheme), Division 2

rules.label: Reasonable and expeditious assessment of suspected eligible data breach
rules.obligation: The entity must carry out a reasonable and expeditious assessment of whether there are reasonable grounds to believe that the relevant circumstances amount to an eligible data breach of the entity.
rules.scope: Applies where an entity is aware of reasonable grounds to suspect an eligible data breach but is not yet aware of reasonable grounds to believe one has occurred.

rules.label: Complete assessment within 30 days
rules.obligation: The entity must take all reasonable steps to ensure the assessment is completed within 30 days after becoming aware of the reasonable grounds to suspect an eligible data breach.
rules.scope: Applies where an entity is aware of reasonable grounds to suspect an eligible data breach but is not yet aware of reasonable grounds to believe one has occurred.

rules.label: Exception for eligible data breaches of other entities
rules.obligation: Where an entity complies with section 26WH in relation to an eligible data breach, and that same access, disclosure or loss is also an eligible data breach of one or more other entities, section 26WH does not apply in relation to those other entities' eligible data breaches.
rules.scope: Entities sharing the same eligible data breach where one entity has already complied with section 26WH

docref.section: Exception—eligible data breaches of other entities

rules.label: Prepare and give statement to Commissioner
rules.obligation: The entity must prepare a statement that complies with subsection (3) and give a copy to the Commissioner as soon as practicable after becoming aware that there are reasonable grounds to believe there has been an eligible data breach.
rules.scope: Applies where an entity is aware of reasonable grounds to believe there has been an eligible data breach of the entity.

rules.label: Required content of statement
rules.obligation: The statement must set out the identity and contact details of the entity, a description of the eligible data breach, the particular kind or kinds of information concerned, and recommendations about steps individuals should take in response.
rules.scope: Applies to the statement prepared under subparagraph (2)(a)(i).

rules.label: Optional inclusion of other affected entities
rules.obligation: If the entity has reasonable grounds to believe the breach is also an eligible data breach of one or more other entities, the statement may also set out the identity and contact details of those other entities.
rules.scope: Applies where the breach also constitutes an eligible data breach of other entities.

rules.label: Notify individuals to whom information relates
rules.obligation: If practicable to notify each individual to whom the relevant information relates, take reasonable steps to notify the contents of the statement to each of those individuals.
rules.scope: Applies where an entity has reasonable grounds to believe an eligible data breach has occurred and has prepared a compliant statement under 26WK(3).
exceptions: Only applies if it is practicable to notify each individual to whom the relevant information relates

rules.label: Notify individuals at risk
rules.obligation: If it is not practicable to notify each individual to whom the information relates but practicable to notify each individual at risk from the breach, take reasonable steps to notify the contents of the statement to each of those at-risk individuals.
rules.scope: Applies where an entity has reasonable grounds to believe an eligible data breach has occurred and has prepared a compliant statement, and paragraph (2)(a) does not apply.
exceptions: Only applies if it is practicable to notify each individual at risk from the breach

rules.label: Publish statement where individual notification impracticable
rules.obligation: If neither paragraph (2)(a) nor (2)(b) applies, publish a copy of the statement on the entity's website (if any) and take reasonable steps to publicise the contents of the statement.
rules.scope: Applies where an entity has reasonable grounds to believe an eligible data breach has occurred and individual notification under (2)(a) and (2)(b) is not practicable.

rules.label: Timing of notification
rules.obligation: The entity must comply with the notification requirement in subsection (2) as soon as practicable after completing the preparation of the statement.
rules.scope: Applies to all entities required to notify an eligible data breach under subsection 26WL(2).

rules.label: Permitted method of providing statement to individual
rules.obligation: Where the entity normally communicates with a particular individual using a particular method, the notification under paragraph (2)(a) or (b) may use that method.
rules.scope: Applies to notifications to individuals under paragraph 26WL(2)(a) or (b).

rules.label: Exception for eligible data breaches of other entities
rules.obligation: Sections 26WK and 26WL do not apply to other entities where one entity has already complied with those sections in relation to the same access, disclosure or loss that constitutes an eligible data breach of those other entities.
rules.scope: Entities sharing an eligible data breach where another entity has complied with notification obligations under sections 26WK and 26WL.

rules.label: Exception for enforcement related activities
rules.obligation: Paragraph 26WK(3)(d) and section 26WL (notification obligations) do not apply where an enforcement body's CEO believes on reasonable grounds that an eligible data breach has occurred and that compliance would likely prejudice one or more enforcement related activities.
rules.scope: Entities that are enforcement bodies where the CEO holds reasonable grounds for belief

rules.label: Exception extends to other affected entities
rules.obligation: Where the access, disclosure or loss constituting the eligible data breach is also an eligible data breach of one or more other entities, the exception in section 26WN applies in relation to those other entities' eligible data breaches as well.
rules.scope: Other entities affected by the same access, disclosure or loss

rules.label: Definition of secrecy provision
rules.obligation: A 'secrecy provision' means a provision of a law of the Commonwealth (other than the Privacy Act) that prohibits or regulates the use or disclosure of information.
rules.scope: Interpretation of secrecy provisions for this section

rules.label: Exception to statement contents where inconsistent with non-prescribed secrecy provision
rules.obligation: If compliance with subparagraph 26WK(2)(a)(ii) would be inconsistent with a secrecy provision (other than a prescribed secrecy provision), subsection 26WK(2) does not apply to the extent of the inconsistency.
rules.scope: Preparation of eligible data breach statements
exceptions: Prescribed secrecy provisions are handled separately under subsections (6)-(7)

rules.label: Exception to notification where inconsistent with non-prescribed secrecy provision
rules.obligation: If compliance with section 26WL would be inconsistent with a secrecy provision (other than a prescribed secrecy provision), section 26WL does not apply to the extent of the inconsistency.
rules.scope: Notification of individuals at risk from eligible data breach
exceptions: Prescribed secrecy provisions are handled separately under subsections (6)-(7)

rules.label: Definition of prescribed secrecy provision
rules.obligation: A 'prescribed secrecy provision' means a secrecy provision specified in the regulations.
rules.scope: Interpretation of prescribed secrecy provisions

rules.label: Prescribed secrecy provisions not treated as authorising use or disclosure
rules.obligation: For the purposes of a prescribed secrecy provision, subparagraph 26WK(2)(a)(ii) and section 26WL are taken not to be provisions that require or authorise the use or disclosure of information.
rules.scope: Interaction of NDB provisions with prescribed secrecy provisions

rules.label: Exception to statement contents where inconsistent with prescribed secrecy provision
rules.obligation: If compliance with subparagraph 26WK(2)(a)(ii) would be inconsistent with a prescribed secrecy provision, subsection 26WK(2) does not apply to the entity in relation to the statement (entirely, not merely to the extent of inconsistency).
rules.scope: Preparation of eligible data breach statements

rules.label: Exception to notification where inconsistent with prescribed secrecy provision
rules.obligation: If compliance with section 26WL would be inconsistent with a prescribed secrecy provision, section 26WL does not apply to the entity in relation to the statement (entirely, not merely to the extent of inconsistency).
rules.scope: Notification of individuals at risk from eligible data breach

rules.label: Commissioner may declare exception from notification obligations
rules.obligation: The Commissioner may, by written notice to the entity, declare that sections 26WK and 26WL do not apply, or that subsection 26WL(3) has effect as if requiring compliance within a specified period, where the Commissioner has reasonable grounds to believe (or is so informed by the entity) that an eligible data breach has occurred.
rules.scope: Eligible data breaches where the Commissioner is aware of or informed of reasonable grounds to believe a breach occurred.

rules.label: Extension of compliance period limited to reasonable period
rules.obligation: The power under paragraph (1)(d) may only be used to extend the time for compliance with subsection 26WL(2) to the end of a period that the Commissioner is satisfied is reasonable in the circumstances.
rules.scope: Declarations extending the compliance period under subsection 26WL(2).

rules.label: Declaration only if reasonable having regard to relevant matters
rules.obligation: The Commissioner must not make a declaration under subsection (1) unless satisfied it is reasonable in the circumstances, having regard to the public interest, any relevant advice from an enforcement body or the Australian Signals Directorate, and such other matters as the Commissioner considers relevant.
rules.scope: All declarations made under subsection 26WQ(1).

rules.label: Declaration on own initiative or on application
rules.obligation: The Commissioner may give notice of a declaration on the Commissioner's own initiative or on application by the entity.
rules.scope: Procedure for initiating declarations.

rules.label: Commissioner may refuse application and must give written notice
rules.obligation: If an entity applies under paragraph (5)(b), the Commissioner may refuse the application and, if so, must give written notice of the refusal to the entity.
rules.scope: Applications by entities for declarations.

rules.label: Specifying different period is not a refusal
rules.obligation: If an application nominates a period but the Commissioner makes the declaration specifying a different period, the Commissioner is taken not to have refused the application.
rules.scope: Applications for paragraph (1)(d) declarations nominating a period.

rules.label: Notification obligations suspended pending decision on application
rules.obligation: If an entity applies under paragraph (5)(b) for a declaration relating to an eligible data breach, sections 26WK and 26WL do not apply to that breach (and related breaches of other entities) until the Commissioner makes a decision on the application.
rules.scope: Period between application and the Commissioner's decision.

rules.label: Entity not entitled to apply if another affected entity has already applied
rules.obligation: An entity is not entitled to make an application under paragraph (5)(b) where the same access, disclosure or loss is an eligible data breach of one or more other entities and one of those other entities has already made an application under paragraph (5)(b).
rules.scope: Multi-entity eligible data breaches arising from the same access, disclosure or loss.

rules.label: Commissioner may extend specified period
rules.obligation: If notice of a paragraph (1)(d) declaration has been given, the Commissioner may, by written notice to the entity, extend the period specified in the declaration.
rules.scope: Existing paragraph (1)(d) declarations specifying a compliance period.

rules.label: Commissioner may direct entity to prepare and provide statement
rules.obligation: If the Commissioner is aware of reasonable grounds to believe there has been an eligible data breach, the Commissioner may by written notice direct the entity to prepare a compliant statement and give a copy to the Commissioner.
rules.scope: Entities subject to NDB scheme where Commissioner has reasonable grounds to believe an eligible data breach occurred

rules.label: Direction must require notification or publication of statement
rules.obligation: The direction must require the entity to notify the statement contents to affected individuals (if practicable), or to at-risk individuals (if practicable), or otherwise to publish the statement on its website and take reasonable steps to publicise it.
rules.scope: Entities receiving a direction under subsection (1)

rules.label: Commissioner must invite submission before giving direction
rules.obligation: Before giving a direction under subsection (1), the Commissioner must invite the entity to make a submission within the specified period.
rules.scope: Commissioner intending to give a direction under 26WR(1)

rules.label: Required contents of statement
rules.obligation: The statement must set out the entity's identity and contact details, a description of the eligible data breach, the kind(s) of information concerned, and recommendations about steps individuals should take.
rules.scope: Statement prepared under direction in 26WR(1)(a)

rules.label: Direction may require additional specified information in statement
rules.obligation: A direction may require the statement to set out specified information relating to the eligible data breach.
rules.scope: Statement prepared under direction in 26WR(1)(a)

rules.label: Commissioner must have regard to relevant matters
rules.obligation: In deciding whether to give a direction, the Commissioner must have regard to relevant advice from enforcement bodies or the Australian Signals Directorate, any relevant submission from the entity, and any other relevant matters.
rules.scope: Commissioner deciding whether to give a direction under 26WR(1)

rules.label: Direction may require identification of other affected entities
rules.obligation: If the Commissioner has reasonable grounds to believe the breach is also an eligible data breach of other entities, the direction may require the statement to set out the identity and contact details of those other entities.
rules.scope: Directions where the breach affects multiple entities

rules.label: Notification may use normal communication method
rules.obligation: If an entity normally communicates with an individual using a particular method, notification under 26WR(2)(a) or (b) may use that method.
rules.scope: Entities notifying individuals under a direction

rules.label: Entity must comply with direction as soon as practicable
rules.obligation: An entity must comply with a direction under subsection (1) as soon as practicable after the direction is given.
rules.scope: Entities given a direction under 26WR(1)

scenarios.label: Unencrypted health records — criminal recipient
scenarios.description: Unauthorised access to unencrypted patient health records by a criminal actor, 5,000+ patients affected

scenarios.label: Encrypted financial database — unknown attacker
scenarios.description: Suspected breach of encrypted financial records; no confirmed access; attacker unknown

scenarios.label: Lost USB — de-identified data
scenarios.description: Physical loss of USB stick containing de-identified employee records

scenarios.label: Ransomware — health + financial, 1000+ individuals
scenarios.description: Ransomware attack on hospital system; health and financial records exfiltrated; 1,000+ patients affected
